summary: A percent is a fraction out of 100. Converting fluently between percent, decimal, and fraction form is the foundation for every percent problem.

formulas.name: Percent to decimal
formulas.notes: Move the decimal point two places left.

formulas.name: Commutativity of percent
formulas.expression: p\% \text{ of } q = q\% \text{ of } p
formulas.notes: Useful shortcut: pick whichever order is easier to compute mentally.

explanation: Percent literally means "per hundred." To convert a percent to a decimal, divide by 100 (move the decimal point two places left): $35\% = 0.35$. To convert a fraction to a percent, convert to a decimal first, then multiply by 100: $\frac{3}{8} = 0.375 = 37.5\%$. To find $p\%$ of a quantity $q$, multiply: $0.p \times q$. A useful mental-math trick is to build percentages from 10% and 1% benchmarks: 10% of a number is found by moving the decimal one place left, 1% by moving it two places left, and other percentages can be built by adding or subtracting these benchmarks (e.g. 15% = 10% + 5%, where 5% is half of 10%).
